symbols.name: k
symbols.def: cohort index of an effect perturbation

symbols.name: s
symbols.def: calendar-period index of an effect perturbation

symbols.name: N
symbols.def: number of units in the Nth panel

symbols.name: n_gN
symbols.def: number of Nth-array units with cohort label g

symbols.name: G_i
symbols.def: adoption cohort of unit i, with exactly n_gN labels equal to g

symbols.name: Y_it(d)
symbols.def: period-t outcome of unit i under treatment state d

symbols.name: Pcal_N
symbols.space: laws on potential-outcome arrays conditional on (G_i)_{i=1}^N

symbols.name: E_Pcal_N
symbols.sig: integrable random variables under Pcal_N -> R

symbols.name: b_iN
symbols.def: unit i untreated baseline mass in array N

symbols.name: gamma_t0
symbols.def: untreated time component, normalized by gamma_10=0

symbols.name: delta_gt
symbols.def: log proportional treatment effect in cohort-time cell (g,t)

symbols.name: v_itN
symbols.role: intercept, unit-FE, time-FE, and treatment regressor

symbols.name: r_gt
symbols.role: collapsed intercept, cohort-FE, time-FE, and treatment regressor

symbols.name: theta_N
symbols.def: coefficient vector on v_itN whose last coordinate is beta

symbols.name: theta
symbols.def: coefficient vector on r_gt whose last coordinate is beta

symbols.name: beta_Nstar(delta)
symbols.def: last coordinate of the unique maximizer of L_N(theta_N;delta)

symbols.name: X_gt
symbols.def: r_gt with its last treatment coordinate removed

symbols.name: rho_star(delta)
symbols.def: argmin_rho sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^T q_gt mu_star_gt(delta)[D_gt-X_gt'rho]^2

symbols.name: hat_beta_N
symbols.def: extended-MLE unit-and-time-FE PPML treatment coefficient with the finite-coordinate convention in def:sample-unit-fe-ppml

symbols.name: R_T
symbols.space: subsets of (simplex shares, baseline masses, effects)
symbols.def: all-positive-effect unit-FE PPML sign-reversal region indexed by (pi_g,B_gt,delta_gt)

symbols.name: W4
symbols.def: the equal-share unit-baseline configuration in def:four-cohort-witness

assumptions.condition: For every N>=|C| and every g in C, n_gN is positive and n_gN/N converges to pi_g in (0,1).
E_Pcal_N[Y_it(0)]=b_iN exp(gamma_t0) for every i and t, with gamma_10=0.

standard.name: unit-fixed-effect multiplicative parallel trends in an exponential mean

assumptions.condition: For every g in C, the deterministic triangular-array average bar_b_gN=n_gN^(-1)sum_{i in I_gN}b_iN converges to bar_b_g in (0,infinity).

novel.justification: This deterministic regularity is met, for example, when the empirical distribution of fixed unit baseline masses within each rollout cohort stabilizes as the cohort grows. It transparently supplies the cohort baseline limit without asserting an unmodeled sampling or mixing law for the fixed array.

assumptions.condition: E_Pcal_N[Y_it(1)]=E_Pcal_N[Y_it(0)] exp(delta_{G_i,t}) for every i and t with D_it=1.
For every N, (NT)^(-1)sum_{i=1}^Nsum_{t=1}^T v_itN v_itN' is positive definite.
sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^T q_gt r_gt r_gt' is positive definite.

novel.justification: Three adoption waves and a never-treated cohort occur in policy panels with several rollout dates and are the smallest scope in which a T>=4 primitive frontier is distinct from the published 2x3 illustration.

Uniformly beneficial proportional effects are empirically meaningful for interventions whose outcome is a nonnegative count or flow; this restriction defines the sign-reversal region rather than an estimation class.

definitions.name: Unit-and-time-FE population PPML projection
definitions.construction: beta_Nstar(delta) is the last coordinate of argmax_{theta_N in R^(N+T)} L_N(theta_N;delta), where L_N(theta_N;delta)=(NT)^(-1)sum_{i=1}^Nsum_{t=1}^T[m_itN(delta)v_itN'theta_N-exp(v_itN'theta_N)].

definitions.name: Collapsed cohort-time population PPML projection
definitions.construction: theta_star(delta)=argmax_{theta in R^(|C|+T)}L(theta;delta), L(theta;delta)=sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^Tq_gt[m_gt(delta)r_gt'theta-exp(r_gt'theta)], beta_star(delta)=the last coordinate of theta_star(delta), and mu_star_gt(delta)=exp(r_gt'theta_star(delta)).

definitions.name: Total extended-MLE unit-and-time-FE PPML coefficient
definitions.construction: Let A_N be the argmax of sum_{i=1}^Nsum_{t=1}^T[Y_it v_itN'theta_N-exp(v_itN'theta_N)] over unit-effect coordinates in R union {-infinity}, time-effect coordinates in R with the period-1 normalization, and beta in R, using exp(-infinity)=0. If A_N contains a point with finite beta, hat_beta_N is its beta coordinate after choosing the finite (time-effect,beta) coordinates of smallest Euclidean norm; otherwise hat_beta_N=0.

definitions.name: Pseudo-true weighted-FWL residual
definitions.construction: rho_star(delta)=argmin_{rho in R^(|C|+T-1)}sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^Tq_gt mu_star_gt(delta)[D_gt-X_gt'rho]^2 and Wtilde_gt(delta)=D_gt-X_gt'rho_star(delta).

definitions.name: All-positive-effect unit-FE PPML sign-reversal region
definitions.construction: R_T={((pi_g)_{g in C},(B_gt)_{g in C,1<=t<=T},(delta_gt)_{(g,t):D_gt=1}): C contains {2,3,4,infinity}; B_gt=bar_b_g exp(gamma_t0); delta_gt>0 whenever D_gt=1; sum_{g,t}q_gt r_gt r_gt' is positive definite; and beta_star(delta)<0}.

definitions.name: Explicit four-cohort sign-reversal witness
definitions.construction: W4 is the cofinal triangular subsequence indexed by N=4m, m=1,2,..., with T=4, C={2,3,4,infinity}, n_2N=n_3N=n_4N=n_infinityN=m, b_iN=1 for every i, gamma_t0=0 for every t, delta_gt=log(101/100) for every treated cell except delta_2,4=log(4).

definitions.name: Primitive-frontier elimination handle
definitions.construction: Starting from the limiting collapsed score equations sum_{g,t}q_gt X_gt[m_gt(delta)-mu_star_gt(delta)]=0 and sum_{g,t}q_gt D_gt[m_gt(delta)-mu_star_gt(delta)]=0, eliminate the intercept, cohort, and time multipliers by their fitted row and column margins, then characterize the beta_star(delta)=0 zero-level set in pi_g=lim_N n_gN/N, B_gt, and delta_gt.

statements.statement: For every N, the unit-and-time-FE population projection in def:unit-fe-population-projection has a unique beta_Nstar(delta). Its beta_Nstar(delta) equals the beta coordinate of the unique maximizer of L_Nc(theta;delta). Consequently, beta_Nstar(delta) converges to beta_star(delta). Thus within-cohort baseline heterogeneity (b_iN) enters the limiting unit-FE coefficient only through B_gtN=n_gN^(-1)sum_{i in I_gN}b_iN exp(gamma_t0).
statements.justification: This anchors the nonlinear geometry to the requested individual-unit and time fixed-effect PPML target while retaining primitive within-cohort baseline heterogeneity.
statements.gap: MoreauKastler2025PTT §3.1 specifies individual and time effects, but does not derive the cohort summary of that unit-FE population projection under heterogeneous unit baseline masses.
statements.consumer: It makes the limiting cohort-time score a faithful representation of the naive unit-and-time-FE PPML estimand rather than a relabelled cohort-FE regression.

statements.statement: The limiting projection theta_star(delta) is unique and satisfies sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^Tq_gt r_gt[m_gt(delta)-mu_star_gt(delta)]=0.
statements.justification: The score characterizes the actual limit established by the unit-FE collapse and supplies the nonlinear geometry used below.
statements.gap: White1982MisspecifiedML supplies generic finite-dimensional pseudo-true-QMLE geometry; this lemma records its Poisson specialization after the unit-FE-to-cohort collapse.
statements.consumer: It supplies the score system differentiated in the forbidden-comparison theorem.

statements.statement: For the limiting coefficient beta_star(delta) of the unit-and-time-FE Poisson projection, at every delta satisfying the assumptions and for every treated cell (k,s), beta_star(delta) is differentiable in delta_ks and d beta_star(delta)/d delta_ks=q_ks B_ks exp(delta_ks) Wtilde_ks(delta)/[sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^Tq_gt mu_star_gt(delta)Wtilde_gt(delta)^2]. The denominator is strictly positive. Hence sign{d beta_star(delta)/d delta_ks}=sign{Wtilde_ks(delta)}, and a treated cell is a forbidden comparison cell if and only if Wtilde_ks(delta)<0. Both Wtilde and its sign depend on delta through mu_star.
statements.justification: This theorem gives the exact local rule: a true proportional effect moves the naive pooled unit-and-time-FE PPML coefficient in the opposite direction if and only if its pseudo-true mean-weighted FWL residual is negative.
statements.gap: MoreauKastler2025PTT provides a 2x3 wrong-comparison calculation, while linear TWFE results use fixed linear weights; neither provides the general effect-dependent PPML derivative identity.
statements.consumer: Applied users can compute the fitted-mean residuals to identify forbidden cohort-time cells, then use the primitive Phi theorem to determine the pooled coefficient's global sign.

statements.statement: The explicitly constructed W4 belongs to R_4. In particular, every treated cell has a strictly positive proportional effect, the exceptional cell (2,4) has the largest effect, and beta_star(delta)<0. At b_iN=1 and delta_gt=0 for all treated cells, Wtilde_gt equals the matrix with rows g=2,3,4,infinity and columns t=1,2,3,4: [(-3,3,1,-1),(-1,-3,3,1),(1,-1,-3,3),(3,1,-1,-3)]/8. Thus Wtilde_2,4=-1/8 and d beta_star(delta)/d delta_2,4<0 at the homogeneous no-effect point; continuity gives an open positive-effect neighborhood in which increasing the late effect of the earliest cohort strictly lowers beta_star.
statements.justification: The explicit T=4 construction proves that the negative-residual derivative rule and an all-positive-effect negative pooled coefficient are both non-vacuous for the actual unit-FE PPML target.
statements.gap: The prior 2x3 illustration does not provide a four-cohort unit-FE witness or locate it relative to a sharp multicohort frontier.
statements.consumer: The witness is a regression-test fixture, and the exact comparison with the 4441/4000 cutoff shows that y=4 is valid but deliberately nonminimal.

statements.statement: If delta_gt=delta_0 for every treated cell, then beta_star(delta)=delta_0 and mu_star_gt(delta)=B_gt exp(D_gt delta_0).
statements.justification: This is the required sanity reduction: a correctly specified unit-and-time-FE PPML coefficient equals the common log proportional effect.
statements.gap: MoreauKastler2025PTT equation (3.5) gives the homogeneous-effect benchmark; it is retained solely as a specification check for the heterogeneous sign theorem.
statements.consumer: It separates heterogeneity-driven forbidden comparisons from normalization or implementation errors.

statements.statement: For every T>=4 and every adoption support C satisfying ass:multicohort-frontier-scope, can the zero-level set {((pi_g),(B_gt),(delta_gt)): beta_star(delta)=0} associated with R_T be characterized by a finite necessary-and-sufficient system of inequalities in the primitive limiting timing shares pi_g=lim_N n_gN/N, baseline masses B_gt, and the full positive effect vector (delta_gt)_{D_gt=1}, without retaining implicit pseudo-true intercept, cohort, or time multipliers?
statements.justification: The fitted-residual iff rule diagnoses a realized panel, but a primitive zero-level characterization would reveal how timing, baselines, and heterogeneous effects jointly permit a unit-FE PPML sign reversal.
statements.gap: GoodmanBacon2021 gives linear comparison weights and deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2020TWFE characterizes negative linear weights; neither eliminates endogenous Poisson margins. MoreauKastler2025PTT §3.1 is a 2x3 calculation, not a finite T>=4 multicohort primitive zero-level characterization.
statements.consumer: A solution would provide design-stage screening rules for cohort composition and baseline imbalance in gravity, tax, and health panels using PPML.

sampling_model.design: A triangular sequence of balanced T-period panels indexed by integers N>=|C|, with deterministic cohort labels, positive counts n_gN for every supported cohort, and unit-specific untreated baseline masses b_iN.
sampling_model.units: Potential outcomes have unit-level exponential untreated means b_iN exp(gamma_t0); within-cohort baseline heterogeneity is allowed, and its deterministic cohort average is imposed to converge to bar_b_g.
sampling_model.treatment: Irreversible adoption D_it=1{G_i<=t}; the frontier scope includes cohorts 2, 3, and 4 plus never-treated units.
sampling_model.overlap: Every limiting cohort share is positive, and both the unit-FE and collapsed intercept/cohort/time/treatment designs have full population rank.
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: The reported estimator is unit-and-time-FE PPML with an extended-MLE convention for zero-outcome or separation realizations; the population target is the unit-FE pseudo-true coefficient whose cohort summary is derived in lem:unit-fe-collapse.

estimand_functional: sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^Tq_gt r_gt[m_gt(delta)-exp(r_gt'theta_star(delta))]=0, with beta_star(delta) the last coordinate of theta_star(delta)
target_estimand: beta_star(delta), the limiting pseudo-true coefficient of naive unit-and-time-fixed-effect PPML under cohort-time proportional effects delta_gt

bibliography.citation: Ninon Moreau-Kastler (2025), Proportional Treatment Effects in Staggered Settings: An Approach for Poisson Pseudo-Maximum Likelihood, EU Tax Observatory Working Paper No. 31, https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.5247936.
Jeffrey M. Wooldridge (2023), Simple Approaches to Nonlinear Difference-in-Differences with Panel Data, The Econometrics Journal 26(3), C31-C66, https://doi.org/10.1093/ectj/utad016.
Andrew Goodman-Bacon (2021), Difference-in-Differences with Variation in Treatment Timing, Journal of Econometrics 225(2), 254-277, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2021.03.014.

Clement de Chaisemartin and Xavier D'Haultfoeuille (2020), Two-Way Fixed Effects Estimators with Heterogeneous Treatment Effects, American Economic Review 110(9), 2964-2996, https://doi.org/10.1257/aer.20181169.
Halbert White (1982), Maximum Likelihood Estimation of Misspecified Models, Econometrica 50(1), 1-25, https://doi.org/10.2307/1912526.
Donald W. K. Andrews (1988), Laws of Large Numbers for Dependent Non-Identically Distributed Random Variables, Econometric Theory 4(3), 458-467, https://doi.org/10.1017/S0266466600013396.

Arne J. Nagengast and Yoto V. Yotov (2025), Staggered Difference-in-Differences in Gravity Settings: Revisiting the Effects of Trade Agreements, American Economic Journal: Applied Economics 17(1), 271-296, https://doi.org/10.1257/app.20230089.

tldr: The project characterizes the actual naive unit-and-time-fixed-effect PPML coefficient in staggered-adoption panels with heterogeneous proportional effects and within-cohort baseline heterogeneity. The unit-FE objective collapses exactly to a cohort-time Poisson projection, and enlarging a cell's true log proportional effect lowers the pooled coefficient exactly when that cell has a negative pseudo-true mean-weighted FWL residual. The global sign is also primitive and explicit: with h_gt=pi_g B_gt exp(D_gt delta_gt), row sums R_g, column sums C_t, total M, and treated total A, sign(beta_star)=sign(Phi) for Phi=M A-sum_{g,t}D_gt R_g C_t. Thus the zero frontier is Phi=0 and the all-positive-effect reversal region is Phi<0. In the equal-share T=4 family, reversal occurs exactly when y>(5*x^2+12*x-15)/2; at x=101/100 the sharp cutoff is 4441/4000, while W4 uses y=4. Operationally, Phi diagnoses when the pooled coefficient is an unsafe causal summary; the identified granular proportional effects and their positive counterfactual-share PTT are a distinct causal target, not a correction of beta_star. No fixed-T sampling or inference theorem is claimed.

project_justification.gap: Moreau-Kastler's two-individual, three-period PPML calculation exposes a wrong-comparison phenomenon but does not give either the general unit-FE derivative identity or a finite multicohort primitive sign frontier. Linear Goodman-Bacon and de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille decompositions cannot supply that frontier because PPML fitted-mean weights depend on the heterogeneous effect vector.
project_justification.niche: Starting from the individual unit-and-time-FE population objective, the analysis allows unit baseline masses to vary within cohorts, collapses them only through cohort averages, and then eliminates the pseudo-true nuisance multipliers by the fitted row and column margins. This produces both a fitted-mean local diagnostic and a design-level primitive sign certificate in timing shares, untreated baseline masses, and the full effect vector.
project_justification.fill: The proved results are the unit-FE collapse, the effect-dependent mean-weighted-FWL derivative rule, a non-vacuous four-cohort reversal, and the exact finite primitive theorem sign(beta_star)=sign(Phi). The T=4 subfamily has the sharp threshold y=(5*x^2+12*x-15)/2, equal to 4441/4000 at x=101/100. When Phi<0 under positive effects, the pooled PPML coefficient is diagnosed as an unsafe summary; researchers may instead report identified granular proportional effects and their counterfactual-share PTT, understood as a different causal estimand rather than a repaired beta_star.

related_work: MoreauKastler2025PTT is the direct conceptual anchor: Section 3.1, including equation (3.6), gives a two-individual, three-period individual-and-time-FE PPML wrong-comparison illustration and explains why proportional effects lack a linear aggregation identity. The present results extend that diagnosis to a general mean-weighted-FWL derivative identity and, after eliminating the endogenous Poisson margins, to the finite primitive certificate sign(beta_star)=sign(Phi). GoodmanBacon2021 supplies the canonical linear staggered-adoption comparison decomposition, and deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2020TWFE establishes negative treatment-cell weights for linear TWFE; both are benchmarks rather than PPML frontier results. NagengastYotov2025 estimates granular cohort-time PPML effects and aggregates them, which supports the diagnose-then-granular-effects strategy but does not reinterpret the misspecified pooled coefficient. Wooldridge2023NonlinearDiD supplies the multiplicative-parallel-trends causal structure, while White1982MisspecifiedML supplies generic pseudo-true-QMLE geometry. None of these references gives the exact Phi frontier or the T=4 threshold proved here.
honest_scope: The contribution is at the observed-population identification and projection level. It proves the unit-FE collapse, the local forbidden-cell derivative rule, the exact primitive global sign frontier Phi=0, and the T=4 reversal threshold, and it identifies a separate positive counterfactual-share PTT from granular proportional effects. Phi is a diagnostic for beta_star, and PTT is a distinct causal estimand; PTT neither corrects nor reinterprets beta_star. The extended sample PPML coefficient is defined on all realizations, but no fixed-T consistency, influence-function, analytical-variance, limit-law, or coverage result is claimed here.
